content.content:
1	//! Coroutine library — port of `lcorolib.c`.
2	//!
3	//! Provides the `coroutine.*` standard-library table: `create`, `resume`,
4	//! `running`, `status`, `wrap`, `yield`, `isyieldable`, and `close`.
5	//!
6	//! # Phase A–D stub notice
7	//!
8	//! Every function that requires actual coroutine execution (`resume`, `yield`,
9	//! cross-thread `xmove`, `new_thread`, `close_thread`) is **unimplemented** and
10	//! will panic at runtime.  The argument-checking and result-packaging logic is
11	//! translated faithfully so that Phase E can drop in the real implementations
12	//! without restructuring.  Phase E wires real stackful coroutines via
13	//! `corosensei`.  See PORTING.md §2 #6.
14	//!
15	//! Translated from: `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/lcorolib.c` (210 lines, 12 functions)
16	//! Target crate: `lua-stdlib`
17	
18	// TODO(port): LuaState, GcRef<LuaState>, LuaStatus, and related types live in
19	// lua-vm / lua-types; all unresolved imports will be fixed in Phase B.
20	use lua_types::{
21	    error::LuaError,
22	    value::LuaValue,
23	    LuaType,
24	    LuaStatus,
25	    gc::GcRef,
26	};
27	use crate::state_stub::{LuaState, LuaStateStubExt as _, lua_CFunction, upvalue_index, CompareOp, LuaDebug};
28	
29	// ── Coroutine status codes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30	
31	// C: #define COS_RUN   0
32	// C: #define COS_DEAD  1
33	// C: #define COS_YIELD 2
34	// C: #define COS_NORM  3
35	
36	/// Coroutine is the currently running thread.
37	const COS_RUN: i32 = 0;
38	
39	/// Coroutine has finished execution or encountered an error.
40	const COS_DEAD: i32 = 1;
41	
42	/// Coroutine is suspended — either yielded or not yet started.
43	const COS_YIELD: i32 = 2;
44	
45	/// Coroutine is normal — it resumed another coroutine and is waiting.
46	const COS_NORM: i32 = 3;
47	
48	/// Human-readable status strings indexed by the `COS_*` constants above.
49	/// Pushed onto the Lua stack as byte strings.
50	///
51	/// C: `static const char *const statname[] = {"running","dead","suspended","normal"};`
52	const STAT_NAMES: [&[u8]; 4] = [b"running", b"dead", b"suspended", b"normal"];
53	
54	// ── Registration table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
55	
56	/// Registration table for the `coroutine` standard library.
57	///
58	/// C: `static const luaL_Reg co_funcs[]`
59	///
60	/// Each entry is `(name_bytes, function_pointer)`. Phase B resolves
61	/// `lua_CFunction` to the canonical type alias from `lua-types`.
62	pub const CO_FUNCS: &[(&[u8], lua_CFunction)] = &[
63	    (b"create",      co_create),
64	    (b"resume",      co_resume),
65	    (b"running",     co_running),
66	    (b"status",      co_status),
67	    (b"wrap",        co_wrap),
68	    (b"yield",       co_yield),
69	    (b"isyieldable", co_isyieldable),
70	    (b"close",       co_close),
71	];
72	
73	// ── Internal helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74	
75	/// Retrieves the coroutine thread at stack index 1, raising a type error if
76	/// the argument is absent or not a thread.
77	///
78	/// C: `static lua_State *getco(lua_State *L)`
79	fn get_co(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<GcRef<lua_types::value::LuaThread>, LuaError> {
80	    let co = state.to_thread(1);
81	    if co.is_none() {
82	        let got = state.arg(1);
83	        return Err(LuaError::type_arg_error(1, "thread", &got));
84	    }
85	    Ok(co.expect("checked above"))
86	}
87	
88	/// Returns one of the `COS_*` status codes describing `co` relative to the
89	/// calling thread `state`.
90	///
91	/// C: `static int auxstatus(lua_State *L, lua_State *co)`
92	fn aux_status(_state: &mut LuaState, _co: &GcRef<lua_types::value::LuaThread>) -> i32 {
93	    // TODO(phase-b): needs lua_vm cross-thread access to status, has_frames,
94	    // get_top, is_same_thread. Phase E wires real coroutines.
95	    todo!("phase-b: coroutine aux_status")
96	}
97	
98	/// Transfers `narg` arguments from `state` to `co`, resumes the coroutine,
99	/// then transfers results (or error message) back to `state`.
100	///
101	/// Returns the number of result values (≥ 0) on success, or `-1` on error
102	/// with the error object left on top of `state`'s stack.
103	///
104	/// C: `static int auxresume(lua_State *L, lua_State *co, int narg)`
105	fn aux_resume(state: &mut LuaState, _co: GcRef<lua_types::value::LuaThread>, _narg: i32) -> i32 {
106	    // Phase A–D stub: real cross-thread resume needs corosensei runtime
107	    // support (PORTING.md §2 #6 — Phase E).  Emulate C's `auxresume` error
108	    // path: push a string error object onto the caller's stack and return
109	    // -1.  `co_resume` packages this as `(false, msg)` and `aux_wrap`
110	    // re-raises it as a Lua error, matching C-Lua semantics on a coroutine
111	    // that cannot run.  Phase E will replace this body with the full
112	    // checkstack / xmove / lua_resume / xmove sequence.
113	    let msg_bytes: &[u8] = b"coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)";
114	    match state.intern_str(msg_bytes) {
115	        Ok(s) => state.push(LuaValue::Str(s)),
116	        Err(_) => state.push(LuaValue::Nil),
117	    }
118	    -1
119	}
120	
121	// ── Public library functions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
122	
123	/// `coroutine.resume(co [, val1, ...])` — attempt to resume coroutine `co`.
124	///
125	/// On success pushes `true` followed by all values yielded or returned by `co`.
126	/// On failure pushes `false` followed by the error object.
127	///
128	/// C: `static int luaB_coresume(lua_State *L)`
129	pub fn co_resume(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
130	    // C: lua_State *co = getco(L);
131	    let co = get_co(state)?;
132	    // C: r = auxresume(L, co, lua_gettop(L) - 1);
133	    // PORT NOTE: lua_gettop returns the argument count; -1 excludes the coroutine
134	    // itself which sits at index 1.
135	    let narg = state.get_top() - 1;
136	    let r = aux_resume(state, co, narg);
137	    if r < 0 {
138	        // C: lua_pushboolean(L, 0); lua_insert(L, -2); return 2;
139	        state.push(LuaValue::Bool(false));
140	        state.insert(-2);
141	        Ok(2)
142	    } else {
143	        // C: lua_pushboolean(L, 1); lua_insert(L, -(r + 1)); return r + 1;
144	        state.push(LuaValue::Bool(true));
145	        state.insert(-(r + 1));
146	        Ok((r + 1) as usize)
147	    }
148	}
149	
150	/// Closure body installed by `coroutine.wrap`.  The wrapped coroutine is
151	/// stored in upvalue slot 1.
152	///
153	/// On error the message is augmented with location info (if a string), then
154	/// re-raised.  If the coroutine is in an error state (not simply suspended),
155	/// its to-be-closed variables are cleaned up before propagation.
156	///
157	/// C: `static int luaB_auxwrap(lua_State *L)`
158	///
159	/// Phase A–D pragmatic implementation: invoke the wrapped function (stored
160	/// in upvalue 1) synchronously on the caller's thread, returning whatever
161	/// it returns.  This works for coroutine bodies that never yield, which
162	/// covers the `calls.lua` tail-call test.  A body that calls
163	/// `coroutine.yield` will surface the Phase-E stub error from `co_yield`.
164	/// Phase E replaces this with the full `auxresume` cross-thread sequence.
165	fn aux_wrap(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
166	    let narg = state.get_top();
167	    state.push_value_at(upvalue_index(1))?;
168	    if narg > 0 {
169	        state.insert(-(narg + 1))?;
170	    }
171	    state.call(narg, -1)?;
172	    Ok(state.get_top() as usize)
173	}
174	
175	/// `coroutine.create(f)` — create a new coroutine that will run function `f`.
176	///
177	/// Pushes the new thread value and returns 1.
178	///
179	/// C: `static int luaB_cocreate(lua_State *L)`
180	pub fn co_create(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
181	    // C: luaL_checktype(L, 1, LUA_TFUNCTION);
182	    state.check_arg_type(1, LuaType::Function)?;
183	    // C: NL = lua_newthread(L);
184	    // TODO(port): coroutine stub — new_thread allocates a fresh LuaState coroutine
185	    // and pushes a Thread value for it; Phase E needed.
186	    let _nl = state.new_thread()?;
187	    // C: lua_pushvalue(L, 1);          /* move function to top */
188	    // C: lua_xmove(L, NL, 1);          /* move function from L to NL */
189	    // PORT NOTE: in C the function copy is pushed and then xmove pops it
190	    // off L's stack into NL's. The Phase E xmove is not yet wired, but the
191	    // net stack effect on L is "thread on top". Skip the push entirely so
192	    // co_wrap's push_cclosure captures the thread (not the function) as
193	    // upvalue 1. Phase E will restore the push + xmove pair.
194	    // TODO(port): coroutine stub — xmove transfers the function from L's stack to
195	    // NL's stack so it becomes the coroutine body; Phase E needed.
196	    Ok(1)
197	}
198	
199	/// `coroutine.wrap(f)` — create a coroutine and return a resuming function.
200	///
201	/// The returned function, when called, resumes the coroutine as if by
202	/// `coroutine.resume`, but raises an error rather than returning `false`.
203	///
204	/// C: `static int luaB_cowrap(lua_State *L)`
205	///
206	/// Phase A–D pragmatic implementation: rather than allocate a (stubbed)
207	/// thread, capture the wrapped function itself as upvalue 1 of `aux_wrap`.
208	/// The closure invokes the function synchronously when called.  Phase E
209	/// restores `luaB_cocreate` + `lua_pushcclosure` once real coroutines exist.
210	pub fn co_wrap(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
211	    state.check_arg_type(1, LuaType::Function)?;
212	    state.push_value_at(1)?;
213	    state.push_cclosure(aux_wrap, 1)?;
214	    Ok(1)
215	}
216	
217	/// `coroutine.yield([...])` — suspend the running coroutine.
218	///
219	/// All arguments are passed back as results of the corresponding `resume`.
220	///
221	/// C: `static int luaB_yield(lua_State *L)`
222	pub fn co_yield(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
223	    // Phase A–D stub: real `lua_yield` needs corosensei stackful coroutines
224	    // (PORTING.md §2 #6 — Phase E).  Raise a Lua error so callers can pcall
225	    // gracefully rather than crashing the host with a Rust panic.
226	    let _nargs = state.get_top();
227	    Err(LuaError::runtime(format_args!(
228	        "coroutine.yield: coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"
229	    )))
230	}
231	
232	/// `coroutine.status(co)` — return a string describing `co`'s current status.
233	///
234	/// Returns one of `"running"`, `"dead"`, `"suspended"`, or `"normal"`.
235	///
236	/// C: `static int luaB_costatus(lua_State *L)`
237	pub fn co_status(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
238	    // C: lua_State *co = getco(L);
239	    let co = get_co(state)?;
240	    // C: lua_pushstring(L, statname[auxstatus(L, co)]);
241	    let idx = aux_status(state, &co) as usize;
242	    let name: &[u8] = STAT_NAMES[idx];
243	    let interned = state.intern_str(name)?;
244	    state.push(LuaValue::Str(interned));
245	    Ok(1)
246	}
247	
248	/// `coroutine.isyieldable([co])` — test whether a coroutine (default: current)
249	/// is in a yieldable state.
250	///
251	/// C: `static int luaB_yieldable(lua_State *L)`
252	pub fn co_isyieldable(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
253	    let is_yieldable = if matches!(state.type_at(1), LuaType::None) {
254	        state.is_yieldable()
255	    } else {
256	        let _co = get_co(state)?;
257	        // TODO(phase-b): needs cross-thread is_yieldable; Phase E.
258	        todo!("phase-b: cross-thread is_yieldable")
259	    };
260	    state.push(LuaValue::Bool(is_yieldable));
261	    Ok(1)
262	}
263	
264	/// `coroutine.running()` — return the current coroutine plus a boolean.
265	///
266	/// The boolean is `true` when the current coroutine is the main thread.
267	///
268	/// C: `static int luaB_corunning(lua_State *L)`
269	pub fn co_running(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
270	    // C: int ismain = lua_pushthread(L);
271	    // TODO(port): push_thread pushes a Thread value for the current LuaState and
272	    // returns true iff it is the main thread; Phase B wire-up needed.
273	    let is_main = state.push_thread()?;
274	    // C: lua_pushboolean(L, ismain);
275	    state.push(LuaValue::Bool(is_main));
276	    Ok(2)
277	}
278	
279	/// `coroutine.close(co)` — close a dead or suspended coroutine.
280	///
281	/// Runs the to-be-closed variable finalizers.  Returns `true` on success, or
282	/// `false` plus an error object on failure.  Raises an error if `co` is
283	/// running or normal.
284	///
285	/// C: `static int luaB_close(lua_State *L)`
286	pub fn co_close(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
287	    let co = get_co(state)?;
288	    let status = aux_status(state, &co);
289	    match status {
290	        s if s == COS_DEAD || s == COS_YIELD => {
291	            // TODO(phase-b): needs cross-thread close_thread + xmove.
292	            todo!("phase-b: coroutine close")
293	        }
294	        _ => {
295	            let name = match status {
296	                COS_RUN => "running",
297	                COS_NORM => "normal",
298	                _ => "unknown",
299	            };
300	            Err(LuaError::runtime(format_args!(
301	                "cannot close a {} coroutine",
302	                name
303	            )))
304	        }
305	    }
306	}
307	
308	// ── Module entry point ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
309	
310	/// Opens the `coroutine` standard library by pushing a new table containing
311	/// all `coroutine.*` functions.
312	///
313	/// C: `LUAMOD_API int luaopen_coroutine(lua_State *L)` — `LUAMOD_API` → `pub`.
314	pub fn open_coroutine(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
315	    // C: luaL_newlib(L, co_funcs);
316	    // TODO(port): state.new_lib(CO_FUNCS) creates a table from the registration
317	    // slice and leaves it on the stack; Phase B wire-up needed.
318	    state.new_lib(CO_FUNCS)?;
319	    Ok(1)
320	}
321	
322	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
323	// PORT STATUS
324	//   source:        src/lcorolib.c  (210 lines, 12 functions)
325	//   target_crate:  lua-stdlib
326	//   confidence:    medium
327	//   todos:         21
328	//   port_notes:    2
329	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
330	//   notes:         All coroutine execution primitives (resume, yield, xmove,
331	//                  new_thread, close_thread) are Phase E stubs that panic.
332	//                  Argument-checking / result-packaging logic is faithfully
333	//                  translated so Phase E can drop in real implementations.
334	//                  The CO_FUNCS table type references lua_CFunction which is
335	//                  resolved in Phase B.  LuaState / GcRef<LuaState> / LuaStatus
336	//                  imports are all deferred to Phase B.
337	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
338

input.command: grep -rn "literals.lua\|coroutine" harness/ 2>&1 | grep -v "\.out:" | grep -v "\.transcript" | grep -v "\.stderr" | grep -v scan- | head -30

content.content:
harness//impl/debug-costs-O11.txt:1:1 1.24946425 reference/lua-c/testes/literals.lua
harness//impl/debug-costs-O10.txt:1:3 1.8579337499999997 reference/lua-c/testes/literals.lua
harness//impl/debug-costs-O13.txt:8:6 7.588594999999998 reference/lua-c/testes/literals.lua
harness//impl/mega-O4-D3-reference_lua_c_testes_gengc_l.translator.json:10:  "result": "No code changes were made. Build remains green (0 errors).

**Report:**

**Root cause:** The test fails at line 102 of gengc.lua: `assert(f() == 123 and ...)`. By bisecting, I confirmed `f` is bound to a **string**, not a function. The string comes from `aux_resume` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:113`, which pushes `"coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"` and returns `-1`. `co_resume` then packages this as `(false, "...")` — valid Lua semantics for resume failure. The test does `local _, f = coroutine.resume(co)` without checking the success boolean, so `f` becomes the error string; calling `f()` later produces `"attempt to call a string value"`.

**This is not a Phase-B bug to fix.** Per PORTING.md §2 #6, coroutines are intentionally stubbed in Phases A–D and require corosensei stackful-coroutine integration in Phase E. `LuaThread` is still a placeholder unit type (`crates/lua-types/src/value.rs:323-327`). Making the test pass requires implementing real coroutines — well out of scope for a single debug invocation.

**Fix:** None applied. The harness misclassified this as `runtime-error` because the stub deliberately *emulates* C's auxresume error path (rather than panicking) so pcall-aware callers can continue. gengc.lua doesn't pcall the resume, so it falls into a misleading downstream error. The test cannot pass until Phase E lands.

**New state:** Test still fails identically — no changes made. Build green.",
harness//impl/mega-O13-D7-reference_lua_c_testes_calls_l.translator.json:10:  "result": "Build is green (warnings only, no errors). I added no temporary instrumentation, so nothing to remove.

**Root cause:** `co_create` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:196-209` translated the C sequence `lua_newthread; lua_pushvalue(1); lua_xmove(L, NL, 1);` but the `xmove` call (which is a Phase E stub) was commented out. So the function copy was pushed but never moved off L's stack — leaving stack as `[func, thread, funcCopy]` with the function on top. Then `co_wrap` calls `push_cclosure(aux_wrap, 1)`, which captured the **function** (not the thread) as upvalue 1, triggering the `to_thread(upvalue_index(1)) → None` panic in `aux_wrap` at line 177.

**Fix:** Removed the stray `push_value(1)` so `co_create` ends with the thread on top of L's stack — matching the C *net effect* after `xmove` would have popped the function. Added a PORT NOTE describing the temporary deviation and leaving the `TODO(port)` for Phase E to restore the push+xmove pair.

**New state:** Not re-tested per instructions. Expected next failure: the test will now reach `aux_resume` which panics with the documented "coroutine.resume is not yet implemented (Phase A–D stub)" — the genuine Phase E gap, no longer masked by the upvalue mis-capture.",
harness//impl/mega-O7-D5-reference_lua_c_testes_nextvar.translator.json:10:  "result": "This is a Phase E feature gap, not a recently-implemented function bug.

**Root cause**: The test at `reference/lua-c/testes/nextvar.lua:393-419` uses `coroutine.wrap` + `coroutine.yield` to test "next x GC of deleted keys". The wrapper's body calls `coroutine.yield(v)` on line 402. Per `PORTING.md §2 #6`, real stackful coroutines are explicitly deferred to Phase E. The current `co_yield` at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:222-230` is an intentional Phase-E stub that raises a Lua runtime error: `"coroutine.yield: coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"`.

The error propagates up correctly through `pcall_k` in the CLI driver — there's no wrong return value, no codegen invariant violation, no missing Phase-B method. The earlier tests (sections 380–387 doing `pairs(t)` + GC of deleted keys without coroutines) print "+ + +" successfully and reach the `print("testing next x GC of deleted keys")` block; only then does the script hit `coroutine.yield`.

**Fix**: None made. The task brief says "REAL bug (not a todo stub)" — this *is* a todo stub by design, and `co_yield`'s body is the documented Phase-E placeholder. Implementing real `coroutine.yield` requires stackful coroutines via `corosensei` (Phase E), which is out of scope for a single Debug-agent invocation and would not be a "real fix" but a multi-day effort.

**No code changes; no instrumentation added; build state unchanged.** This test cannot pass until Phase E lands. The mega-loop should classify `nextvar.lua` as Phase-E-blocked rather than dispatching it as a fixable runtime bug.",
harness//impl/debug-costs-O14.txt:2:4 1.9597812499999996 reference/lua-c/testes/literals.lua
harness//impl/mega-O6-D5-reference_lua_c_testes_calls_l.translator.json:10:  "result": "Build is clean. Per instructions I'm not running the program — the mega loop will re-test.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:158-181` — `aux_wrap` called `aux_resume`, a Phase-A–D stub that unconditionally pushed `"coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"` and returned -1 (lines 113-118), causing `coroutine.wrap(function() return foo() end)()` at `calls.lua:187` to error out. `co_create` only pushed a placeholder `LuaThread`, so the wrap closure had a useless thread as upvalue 1.

**The fix:** Replaced the stub path with a pragmatic synchronous wrapper. `co_wrap` now captures the wrapped function itself as upvalue 1 (instead of routing through `co_create`'s placeholder thread). `aux_wrap` now reads the function from upvalue 1, splices it before the args via `push_value_at` + `insert`, and calls it with `LUA_MULTRET`, returning whatever the function returns. This handles every coroutine body that never calls `yield` — covering the lone `calls.lua` use whose intent (`-- to ensure stack is not preallocated`) is moot in Rust since we have one shared stack. A body that does yield will surface the existing Phase-E error from `co_yield`. Phase E will restore the real `cocreate` + `xmove` + `auxresume` sequence.

**Post-fix state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. I did not re-run the test (per instructions). The previous failure at the `coroutine.wrap` line should be gone; whether the rest of `calls.lua` passes depends on tests beyond line 187, which the mega loop will surface.",
harness//impl/gengc_part.lua:1:local co = coroutine.create(
harness//impl/gengc_part.lua:8:    x = coroutine.yield(f)
harness//impl/gengc_part.lua:10:    coroutine.yield()
harness//impl/gengc_part.lua:14:local ok, f = coroutine.resume(co)
harness//impl/iter-2-debug-2.translator.json:10:  "result": "The test now succeeds cleanly — quicksort runs to completion and prints the sorted array `1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 9`.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/func.rs:691` — `state_in_twups()` was a hard-coded stub returning `false`. The debug-assert at `find_upval` (line 224) checks `state_in_twups(state) || state.openupval.is_empty()`, so any second call to `find_upval` (or any call when `openupval` was non-empty) tripped the assert. In the quicksort program, the recursive Lua closures `partition` and `quicksort` capture upvalues, triggering `find_upval` on the populated list during the recursive descent.

**Fix:** Flipped `state_in_twups` to return `true`. The C `isintwups` invariant is a structural artifact of the C intrusive linked-list design (where `L->twups == L` is a sentinel meaning "not in list"). Our Vec-based design with stubbed coroutines (Phase A–D) has effectively one thread, and the actual `GlobalState.twups` Vec management is already deferred via `TODO(port)` at line 207-211. Treating the thread as conceptually always-in-twups satisfies the invariant without affecting any real behaviour, because nothing currently consumes the twups list. Updated the doc comment to a `PORT NOTE` explaining this.

**New status:** No new error — the test now passes and prints the sorted output `1 2 3 5 8 9` followed by `[ok] execution completed, status=Ok`.

No temporary `eprintln!` was added (the call path was clear from the panic message), so nothing to remove.",
harness//impl/mega-O14-D4-reference_lua_c_testes_literal.translator.json:10:  "result": "Build is green (warnings only, no errors).

## Report

**Root cause**: `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:129` in `aux_resume`, reached via the for-loop iteration of `coroutine.wrap(...)` at literals.lua:291. The previous debug pass (O13-D6) had already fixed the real bugs in this test (`%p` formatting + `intern_or_create_str` long-string handling). What remains is the deliberate Phase-A–D coroutine stub per PORTING.md §2 #6 — this test cannot pass without real stackful coroutines (Phase E corosensei work), which is out of scope.

The actionable bug: the stub was using `panic!("…is not yet implemented…")` with a free-form message that does **not** match the harness's stub-detection regex (`not yet implemented: <slug>:`). The harness mis-classified it as a `runtime-panic`, so it kept dispatching debug agents on this terminal-Phase-E blocker instead of routing it through the stub queue (where it would be recognized as Phase-E and left alone).

**Fix**: Converted the bare `panic!` in `aux_resume` and `co_yield` to `todo!("phase-e: coroutine_resume — …")` / `todo!("phase-e: coroutine_yield — …")`. `todo!()` produces a panic message of the form `not yet implemented: phase-e: coroutine_resume`, which the harness's `surface_scan` regex matches as a stub blocker.

**New state**: Not re-tested (per instructions). The literals.lua test will still fail at the same code path — but now categorized as a `phase-e:` stub rather than a runtime panic, so the harness routes it correctly. `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green.",
harness//impl/mega-O12-1-new_thread_.translator.json:10:  "result": "Build is green. Summary:

**Target:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs:289` — `new_thread()` trait default (`todo!("phase-b-reconcile: new_thread")`).

**What `new_thread` does:** Creates a new Lua coroutine thread sharing the caller's `GlobalState`; per C `lua_newthread`, it pushes the thread value onto the caller's stack and returns the thread. The Rust implementation delegates to `lua_vm::state::new_thread` (which already implements the C body), then retrieves the just-pushed thread via `lua_vm::api::to_thread(self, -1)`.

**Family members implemented in this session:**
- `new_thread()` — calls `lua_vm::state::new_thread`, returns the `GcRef<LuaThread>` it pushed.
- `is_same_thread(other)` — pointer-identity comparison on the two `LuaState` references (`std::ptr::eq`), matching C's `L == co`.

Both added as overrides inside the `impl LuaStateStubExt for LuaState` block (lines 956–966) so Rust's inherent/impl resolution silences the trait `todo!` defaults.

**Other thread-family slots already covered upstream:** `thread_status`, `to_thread`, `to_thread_at`, `push_thread` — all already implemented in the impl block, so no further family work needed.

**Newly-surfaced todos noticed but NOT implemented (different family):**
- `lua_vm::state::new_thread` itself still has TODO(port) notes around GC registration, hook sharing, `extra_space` copy, and the `LuaValue::Thread` placeholder unification — these belong to `lua-vm`, out of scope here.
- The `coro_lib.rs:92` `aux_status` body remains stubbed pending cross-thread API.

**Build:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, no errors).",

Recent commits (`ac6ef46`, `dbf7b41`, `c81cdea`, etc.) appear to have fixed it between when the failure tail was captured and now.

- **Current failure mode** is different: the test now progresses past the long-string interning block and the line-end/long-bracket tests, then hits the explicit Phase E coroutine stub at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:113` (`aux_resume` → `"coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"`), triggered by `coroutine.wrap` at literals.lua:291. Per `PORTING.md §2 rule #6`, coroutines are intentionally stubbed in Phases A–D, so this is a known TODO stub, not a real bug. The task instructions explicitly direct me NOT to fix todo stubs.

- **Fix:** None. No code changes made; build remains green (`cargo build -q -p lua-cli` succeeds).

harness//impl/mega-O7-D2-reference_lua_c_testes_nextvar.translator.json:10:  "result": "No code changes needed; reporting findings.

## Report

**Root cause** — There is **no real bug** in this iteration. The failure `Runtime: coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)` originates at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:113-118` (the `aux_resume` Phase A–D stub). It is hit by `nextvar.lua:393` where the test does `local co = coroutine.wrap(function (t) ... coroutine.yield(v) ... end)` and then iterates `while co(t) do …`. Resuming the wrapped coroutine genuinely requires the corosensei-based stackful coroutine runtime, which is explicitly deferred to Phase E per PORTING.md §2 #6.

**Fix** — None to apply. The previous debug iteration (commit `af4028f`) already fixed the real bug that was blocking earlier in the file (`raw_set` turning into `swap_remove` corrupting hash-slot positions, and `next_pair`/`LuaTableRefExt::next` mis-reporting "invalid key" for nil-valued entries). Those edits to `crates/lua-types/src/value.rs` (`contains_key` at line 191, `next_pair` at 215) and `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs::next` are still in place and working — the test now advances all the way through the prior "erasing values" / "GC of deleted keys" assertions and only stops where it is supposed to: a `coroutine.wrap` call. Implementing minimal-fake coroutines that return nil/empty would paper over the issue *and* still fail the `assert(count == 0 …)` on `nextvar.lua:418`, so it is not a viable shortcut.

**New state** — Output is identical to the previous iteration (13 lines, stops at `coroutine.wrap`). `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean. No temporary instrumentation was added. This test will remain blocked until Phase E lands real coroutines.",
harness//impl/mega-O4-D5-reference_lua_c_testes_calls_l.translator.json:10:  "result": "Build is green. My eprintln has been reverted.

## Report

**Root cause:** This is not a fixable bug at the current phase — it's a Phase E gate. The test reaches line 187 (`assert(coroutine.wrap(function() return foo() end)() == 1023)` in `reference/lua-c/testes/calls.lua`) which genuinely requires coroutine execution. The error message originates from the deliberate Phase A–D stub at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:113` (`aux_resume`), which per PORTING.md §2 #6 ("Coroutines stubbed in A–D") returns `-1` with the "coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)" sentinel.

**Investigation:** I instrumented `aux_resume` with an eprintln to confirm the call site. The output showed it triggered exactly once — after the C-stack-overflow `xpcall(loop, loop)` test (line 165) successfully unwound through many `func::close` invocations and the test reached line 187. Every line 1–186 executed correctly; the test is fundamentally Phase-E-gated.

**Fix:** None made. The Phase A–D stub behavior is correct per the port strategy. I removed the temporary eprintln I added; no other code changes. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is green.

**New state:** Identical to before — the test still fails with the same coroutine-not-implemented error. The mega-loop's stuck-detect should classify this as `same sig 2 rounds` and skip it on the next pass (as already happened for `literals.lua`, which has the same signature).",

**Report:**

**Root cause:** The test fails at line 102 of gengc.lua: `assert(f() == 123 and ...)`. By bisecting, I confirmed `f` is bound to a **string**, not a function. The string comes from `aux_resume` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:113`, which pushes `"coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"` and returns `-1`. `co_resume` then packages this as `(false, "...")` — valid Lua semantics for resume failure. The test does `local _, f = coroutine.resume(co)` without checking the success boolean, so `f` becomes the error string; calling `f()` later produces `"attempt to call a string value"`.

**This is not a Phase-B bug to fix.** Per PORTING.md §2 #6, coroutines are intentionally stubbed in Phases A–D and require corosensei stackful-coroutine integration in Phase E. `LuaThread` is still a placeholder unit type (`crates/lua-types/src/value.rs:323-327`). Making the test pass requires implementing real coroutines — well out of scope for a single debug invocation.

**Fix:** None applied. The harness misclassified this as `runtime-error` because the stub deliberately *emulates* C's auxresume error path (rather than panicking) so pcall-aware callers can continue. gengc.lua doesn't pcall the resume, so it falls into a misleading downstream error. The test cannot pass until Phase E lands.

**New state:** Test still fails identically — no changes made. Build green.",
harness//impl/mega-O13-D7-reference_lua_c_testes_calls_l.translator.json:10:  "result": "Build is green (warnings only, no errors). I added no temporary instrumentation, so nothing to remove.

**Root cause:** `co_create` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:196-209` translated the C sequence `lua_newthread; lua_pushvalue(1); lua_xmove(L, NL, 1);` but the `xmove` call (which is a Phase E stub) was commented out. So the function copy was pushed but never moved off L's stack — leaving stack as `[func, thread, funcCopy]` with the function on top. Then `co_wrap` calls `push_cclosure(aux_wrap, 1)`, which captured the **function** (not the thread) as upvalue 1, triggering the `to_thread(upvalue_index(1)) → None` panic in `aux_wrap` at line 177.

**Fix:** Removed the stray `push_value(1)` so `co_create` ends with the thread on top of L's stack — matching the C *net effect* after `xmove` would have popped the function. Added a PORT NOTE describing the temporary deviation and leaving the `TODO(port)` for Phase E to restore the push+xmove pair.

**New state:** Not re-tested per instructions. Expected next failure: the test will now reach `aux_resume` which panics with the documented "coroutine.resume is not yet implemented (Phase A–D stub)" — the genuine Phase E gap, no longer masked by the upvalue mis-capture.",
harness//impl/mega-O7-D5-reference_lua_c_testes_nextvar.translator.json:10:  "result": "This is a Phase E feature gap, not a recently-implemented function bug.

**Root cause**: The test at `reference/lua-c/testes/nextvar.lua:393-419` uses `coroutine.wrap` + `coroutine.yield` to test "next x GC of deleted keys". The wrapper's body calls `coroutine.yield(v)` on line 402. Per `PORTING.md §2 #6`, real stackful coroutines are explicitly deferred to Phase E. The current `co_yield` at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:222-230` is an intentional Phase-E stub that raises a Lua runtime error: `"coroutine.yield: coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"`.

The error propagates up correctly through `pcall_k` in the CLI driver — there's no wrong return value, no codegen invariant violation, no missing Phase-B method. The earlier tests (sections 380–387 doing `pairs(t)` + GC of deleted keys without coroutines) print "+ + +" successfully and reach the `print("testing next x GC of deleted keys")` block; only then does the script hit `coroutine.yield`.

**Fix**: None made. The task brief says "REAL bug (not a todo stub)" — this *is* a todo stub by design, and `co_yield`'s body is the documented Phase-E placeholder. Implementing real `coroutine.yield` requires stackful coroutines via `corosensei` (Phase E), which is out of scope for a single Debug-agent invocation and would not be a "real fix" but a multi-day effort.

**No code changes; no instrumentation added; build state unchanged.** This test cannot pass until Phase E lands. The mega-loop should classify `nextvar.lua` as Phase-E-blocked rather than dispatching it as a fixable runtime bug.",

harness//impl/mega-O6-D5-reference_lua_c_testes_calls_l.translator.json:10:  "result": "Build is clean. Per instructions I'm not running the program — the mega loop will re-test.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:158-181` — `aux_wrap` called `aux_resume`, a Phase-A–D stub that unconditionally pushed `"coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"` and returned -1 (lines 113-118), causing `coroutine.wrap(function() return foo() end)()` at `calls.lua:187` to error out. `co_create` only pushed a placeholder `LuaThread`, so the wrap closure had a useless thread as upvalue 1.

**The fix:** Replaced the stub path with a pragmatic synchronous wrapper. `co_wrap` now captures the wrapped function itself as upvalue 1 (instead of routing through `co_create`'s placeholder thread). `aux_wrap` now reads the function from upvalue 1, splices it before the args via `push_value_at` + `insert`, and calls it with `LUA_MULTRET`, returning whatever the function returns. This handles every coroutine body that never calls `yield` — covering the lone `calls.lua` use whose intent (`-- to ensure stack is not preallocated`) is moot in Rust since we have one shared stack. A body that does yield will surface the existing Phase-E error from `co_yield`. Phase E will restore the real `cocreate` + `xmove` + `auxresume` sequence.

**Post-fix state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. I did not re-run the test (per instructions). The previous failure at the `coroutine.wrap` line should be gone; whether the rest of `calls.lua` passes depends on tests beyond line 187, which the mega loop will surface.",

delta.text:
## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:222-230
` — `co_yield` is a deliberate Phase A–D stub that returns `LuaError::runtime("coroutine.yield: coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E

)")`. It is reached via `literals.lua:282-293`:

```lua
local function gen (c, n)
  if n==0 then coroutine.

yield(c)
  ...
for s in coroutine.wrap(function () gen("", len) end) do
```
`coroutine.wrap` returns the synchronous `aux_wrap` closure (the pragmatic stub from
`coro_lib.rs:165-173`). The for-loop calls it, which runs `gen("", 4)` recursively in
-line; the first leaf call invokes `coroutine.yield(c)` → Phase-E stub raises →
propagates through `pcall_k` in `lua-cli/src/main.rs:140`. The earlier 4 `
+` prints (lines 143/157/163/172/203) all succ
eeded, confirming the scanner / long-string / locale / line-end

portions of the test are intact.

**Fix:** None applied. Per `POR
TING.md §2 #6` coroutines are explicitly deferred to Phase E (corosensei st
ackful coroutines). This is documented expected behavior, not a rec
ently-implemented function with a wrong return value. Five prior debug agents (O5-

D5, O7-D2, O7-D5, O4-D5, O14-D4) reached the same conclusion in the harness logs.

**New
state:** No code changes; `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only). Test will
fail identically until Phase E lands. No temporary instrumentation was added (call
path was unambiguous from the error string).

content.text:
## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:222-230` — `co_yield` is a deliberate Phase A–D stub that returns `LuaError::runtime("coroutine.yield: coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)")`. It is reached via `literals.lua:282-293`:

```lua
local function gen (c, n)
  if n==0 then coroutine.yield(c)
  ...
for s in coroutine.wrap(function () gen("", len) end) do
```

`coroutine.wrap` returns the synchronous `aux_wrap` closure (the pragmatic stub from `coro_lib.rs:165-173`). The for-loop calls it, which runs `gen("", 4)` recursively in-line; the first leaf call invokes `coroutine.yield(c)` → Phase-E stub raises → propagates through `pcall_k` in `lua-cli/src/main.rs:140`. The earlier 4 `+` prints (lines 143/157/163/172/203) all succeeded, confirming the scanner / long-string / locale / line-end portions of the test are intact.

**Fix:** None applied. Per `PORTING.md §2 #6` coroutines are explicitly deferred to Phase E (corosensei stackful coroutines). This is documented expected behavior, not a recently-implemented function with a wrong return value. Five prior debug agents (O5-D5, O7-D2, O7-D5, O4-D5, O14-D4) reached the same conclusion in the harness logs.

**New state:** No code changes; `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only). Test will fail identically until Phase E lands. No temporary instrumentation was added (call path was unambiguous from the error string).
